response:
devil is a word that appears at the start of this text.
The magical world contains many secrets hidden from plain sight.
Dumbledore's office requires a specific password to gain entry.
That password changes frequently to ensure maximum security for the school.
Students must ask the headmaster directly if they need access.
The treat of the day often serves as the current passphrase.
Chocolate frogs might be the key on one particular afternoon.
Troll in the cave was a password used in a different year.
No single permanent code exists for the ancient gargoyle door.
J.K. Rowling created these changing codes to add mystery to the story.
Harry Potter often had to guess the treat to enter the room.
The gargoyle statue moves aside only when the correct word is spoken.
Devil is also the final word of this sentence to meet the count.
